Article Title: Jamie Dimon: CEO of America’s biggest bank Jamie Dimon on why making decisions on a Friday is a bad idea: ‘They never …’
Article Text:
Jamie Dimon: ‘Anger doesn’t help’





Jamie Dimon, CEO of America's biggest bank JPMorgan Chase, has a simple message for anyone planning an important decision: don’t do it when you are tired. Speaking in an interview with NPR’s “Newsmakers” recently, Jamie Dimon said, “Making big decisions on a Friday when you're tired is a really bad idea. ” Dimon’s advice is linked to what experts call decision fatigue. By the end of the week, people often make many decisions without enough time to rest, which affects their thinking.Thomas Roulet, a professor of organizational sociology and leadership at the University of Cambridge, told Business Insider that Dimon’s view is right. He said that “cognitive resources — a CEO's ability to juggle and process all information they have to make the right decision — are depleted by Friday.” He added that people may also rush decisions as the week ends, without having all the information or time to consult others."As a CEO, if you have taken decisions throughout the week without time to recharge, cognitive resources — a CEO's ability to juggle and process all information they have to make the right decision — are depleted by Friday," Roulet said.During the interaction, Jamie Dimon highlighted emotional control as an important skill for leaders.“Anger doesn't help,” he said, noting that emotions can affect judgment. He added that these are lessons he has “learned and relearned” over time. “I still make some of those mistakes, unfortunately,” he said.Dimon also spoke about values beyond work. He said he was raised to “have a purpose in life, treat everyone well, do the best you can, leave the world a better place.” He also explained the meaning of a well-known phrase. “When they said the pursuit of happiness, they didn't mean happiness like we mean happiness,” he said, adding that it really meant “purpose.”According to Jamie Dimon, purpose can come from many roles. “That purpose could be an artist, politician, reporter, you know, business person,” he said. “You could be just a caregiver, a mother.”He also recalled reading an op-ed about a Medal of Honor recipient who later felt that the real heroes were those who quietly helped others every day. "They never gave up, and they did it through health and sickness and things like that," Jamie Dimon said. "So that's the purpose. You made the world a better place in the way you can contribute," he added.
Article Summary: Jamie Dimon, CEO of JPMorgan Chase, said making big decisions on a Friday when you're tired is a bad idea. His advice is linked to what experts call decision fatigue. By the end of the week, people often make many decisions without enough time to rest.

Article Title: “Three-against-one situation”: El Rubius opens up on being “targeted” in MrBeast’s viral $1M challenge
Article Text:
El Rubius reacted honestly after his elimination from MrBeast’s $1 million challenge, calling it “unfair” and claiming he was targeted in a three-against-one situation. Despite his frustration, he thanked fans and gave away his $50,000 winnings. His response felt real and balanced, especially after MrBeast’s team later sent him a cake, softening the moment.

El Rubius reflects on a tough and “unfair” exit







El Rubius did not just walk away from MrBeast’s $1 million streamer challenge. He opened up. Soon after his elimination, the Spanish creator shared a raw reaction with fans, giving them a closer look at what the experience felt like from inside the game. It was not polished or carefully framed. It felt real, and that is exactly why it caught attention so quickly.The clip and his response have since fueled conversations across the streaming space. Some viewers believe intense competitions like this naturally lead to uneven situations. Others feel Rubius may have had a point about being singled out. Either way, his words have added a human layer to a challenge that many only saw as entertainment.Speaking through an Instagram Story, Rubius did not hide his frustration, but he also made sure to thank his supporters. He said, "Guys, I just got knocked out of the game. It was a bit of an unfair elimination, but hey, at least I managed to walk away with $50,000 for you all, which I’ve already distributed. It went to the mods, the subs, and the non-subs alike."He further added, "I’m happy, of course, though admittedly, it was incredibly tough.I wasn't really a fan of the challenge itself, either. But then again... it was basically a three-against-one situation the entire time. I mean, they were targeting me specifically the whole way through. I kept trying to go after RaKai, but, well, he actually just got eliminated in the end. So, at least justice was served for Abraham. Abraham, if you’re watching this, we were all rooting for you, and standing right there with you."His message carried mixed emotions. There was disappointment, yes, but also pride in how he handled things. Giving away his $50,000 winnings to his community stood out as a gesture that meant more than the result itself.He continued with another honest note, saying, "And in the end, karma finally caught up with that little punk. Sorry, I’m being a bit toxic right now, but let's just hope the MrBeast team doesn't see this! Guys, thank you all so much. Seriously, thank you for all the support and for just being there."He added, "Every time I asked for something in the chat or whatever it was, you guys were right there. You’re incredible. I’m a little disappointed in myself, personally, mostly for not performing better, but hey, I gave it my all, and we still walked away with $50,000, so we’ve got to look on the bright side. All right, guys, big hugs to all my brothers out there!"Interestingly, the story did not end with frustration. Later that day, Rubius revealed that MrBeast’s team had sent him a cake after his exit. It was a small gesture, but it shifted the mood. It showed that even in a high-pressure challenge, there is still room for respect.In the end, Rubius’ reaction felt honest and grounded. That is what stayed with people.
Article Summary: El Rubius was eliminated from MrBeast's $1 million streamer challenge. He called it "unfair" and said he was targeted in a three-against-one situation. Despite his frustration, he thanked fans and gave away his $50,000 winnings.

Article Title: Ipl Ticket Scam: IPL craze costs techie Rs 1.46 lakh in fake RCB vs CSK ticket scam
Article Text:
Image credit: AI generated pic

Watch Devdutt Padikkal press conference: Chinnaswamy pitch, Tim David’s power & RCB’s batting strategy Devdutt Padikkal press conference: Chinnaswamy pitch, Tim David’s power & RCB’s batting strategy

RCB register their second win

Poll Do you think the demand for IPL tickets is too high every season? Yes, it's excessive. No, it's just right.

NEW DELHI: IPL comes and the demand for tickets and passes go soaring up by the fans. The desperation to get inside the stadium to watch their favourite teams in action in the IPL go high and high. When the tickets get sold out, the demand gets too hig, and the cricket fans even ask for tickets in black. But for a 25-year-old techie the craze for IPL tickets for RCB vs CSK turned too costly and he was duped for a massive Rs. 1.46 lakh.The techie, a resident of B Narayanapura, fell into a trap by a post posted by a fraudster who posed himself as a ticket seller on Instagram.As per a report in Deccan Herald, the techie alleged in a complaint to Mahadevapura Police that an individual claiming to be Sumit Biswal promised he could arrange IPL tickets along with food coupons.As per the techie, Biswal was introduced him as a senior supervisor for ticket counter at M Chinnaswamy Stadium"He asked me to come near the stadium gate number 10, saying someone would deliver the tickets within minutes, and even sent an email confirmation to gain my trust," the complainant told Deccan Herald."Initially, I agreed to buy two tickets for Rs 3,700 each,but he kept asking for more money under various pretexts such as refundable security deposits, additional ID cards and food coupons.Trusting him, I made multiple payments - even using my mother’s bank account after exhausting my own limits—and ended up transferring around Rs 1.46 lakh," he added.Bhuvneshwar Kumar’s incisive spell of 3-41, coming after a marauding batting show from Tim David, Rajat Patidar and Devdutt Padikkal, propelled Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) to a commanding 43-run victory over Chennai Super Kings (CSK) in their IPL 2026 encounter at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium on Sunday.The contest saw RCB’s batting unit dismantle CSK’s attack with relentless hitting, as David’s 70 not out off just 25 balls and Patidar’s unbeaten 48 off only 19 balls provided for the late fireworks after Padikkal’s fluent 50 off 29 balls had set the tone for the daunting total.Their combined effort lifted RCB to a daunting 250/3, thus setting a new record for highest total in IPL 2026. In reply, CSK faltered under scoreboard pressure, with Sarfaraz Khan’s 50 off 24 balls and Prashant Veer’s 43 the lone acts of resistance amid a string of failures from the top order.Bhuvneshwar was at his disciplined best and even crossed the 200-mark in terms of wickets in IPL, with others also chipping in as CSK were bowled out for 207 in 19.4 overs. The comprehensive win also meant RCB have registered four consecutive triumphs over CSK for the first time in the IPL’s history.
Article Summary: Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) beat Chennai Super Kings (CSK) in their IPL 2026 encounter at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium on Sunday. Tim David, Rajat Patidar and Devdutt Padikkal put on a marauding batting show to lift RCB to a daunting 250/3. RCB have registered four consecutive triumphs over CSK for the first time in the IPL's history.

Article Title: Clavicular Viral Controversy: "Trans women are.....": Clavicular’s viral moment with trans women sparks fresh conversation on internet culture
Article Text:
A viral clip featuring Clavicular and trans women is gaining attention for its calm and honest tone. The conversation explores how online language and trends move across communities. Instead of conflict, the exchange highlights shared influence and mutual awareness. Viewers are engaging with its thoughtful nature, as it reflects how internet culture evolves through collaboration rather than ownership.

Clavicular and trans women share a surprising realization about online trends







The video of Clavicular, a short-video producer, is going around the Internet, yet not the reason why. There is no yelling or over-the-top drama. It is a mere, everyday talk that seems almost novel nowadays, in the age of internet, which is overflowing with content. The scene is easy-going, the tone of the narrative is straightforward, and the characters appear more concerned with conversation than combat.The message that is being addressed is the flow of the conversation. It discusses internet culture, the way people communicate with one another and the place of trends. Rather than bringing out a debate, the encounter towards curiosity. It quietly brings attention to something that a lot of people who use the internet notice but don't often say out loud. People in different communities often have a bigger impact on each other than they think.The conversation begins with a confident and lighthearted comment from one of the trans women present. She says, "You know that trans women are like the OG lip masters, right? We are very talented in the program. Like, I do the peptides, I'm on Zeta." Her tone carries a mix of pride and humor, setting a relaxed vibe at the table.Clavicular responds in a way that keeps the mood steady but adds a different angle.He says, "That is funny because you guys were using a lot of the words in like the South Korean subreddits in like 2014." It’s not confrontational. If anything, it feels like an observation. He points to how certain words and ideas may have traveled across different online spaces over time.Another voice joins in and brings the moment full circle. "Guys, we're like the OGs. I saw a couple of posts about that. We're like copying each other." That line hits in a simple but honest way. It reflects something familiar. Online culture is rarely created in isolation. It grows through sharing, borrowing, and sometimes even unknowingly repeating.That's what makes the clip so powerful. It doesn't want to make a point or win an argument. It only shows a group of people talking, listening, and figuring something out together.This moment feels different in a space where viral content often leads to fights. It reminds people that conversations can be calm, thoughtful, and still important. And sometimes, that honest silence is what makes it work.
Article Summary: A viral clip featuring Clavicular and trans women is gaining attention for its calm and honest tone. The conversation explores how online language and trends move across communities. Viewers are engaging with its thoughtful nature, as it reflects how internet culture evolves through collaboration.

Article Title: Sykkuno scandal deepens as multiple women come forward with shocking allegations
Article Text:
Allegations against Sykkuno have triggered a major online backlash, with multiple women sharing personal experiences that paint a troubling picture. From past relationships to uncomfortable encounters, several voices claim patterns of dishonesty and manipulation. While supporters remain divided, the streamer has not yet publicly responded. As the story gains traction, the controversy continues to grow, raising questions about accountability, influence, and trust within the streaming community.

Streaming star Sykkuno accused of manipulation as claims flood social media







The streaming world was rudely awakened by the allegations against Sykkino that were breaking out like a dam on social media, and which tugged at the veil of a well-cultivated online identity. What one paper was supposed to be soon became a torrent of opinion, and each one contributed a new twist to a narrative which is by no means complete. The allegations, grave and very personal, have not been addressed publicly so far by the streamer, creating a vacuum that is filled by speculation, praise, and a strong polarization among fans and fellow creators.The most recent addition to the list of women who claim their experience with the popular content creator was not as good as what viewers were watching on the screen is in the middle of it all. Although all the accusations have not been confirmed by a court, the uniformity of tone and timing has taken the discussion into the limelight and the streaming community is now forced to ask itself some uncomfortable questions about power, trust, and responsibility.One of the earliest voices came from Lena, who wrote:"I dated him from ~2015-2019, I warned a few people about him at the time but most ppl didn't believe me (and got harassed for it).Does anyone know how I can get in contact with his gf (if she will speak with me)? I rly want to talk to her 🥺 I feel bad that so many ppl got hurt."Her claims opened the door for others. VTuber Elfilea shared her emotional reaction:"I am beyond disappointed and heartbroken. After 5 years of knowing each other, I had really grown to trust him, only for him to take advantage of that, of me. I feel used and lied to."Some accounts describe uncomfortable encounters rather than long-term relationships.Dotty recalled:"I met Sykkuno at my first TwitchCon in 2022. He was on a party bus, asked me if I knew what a “happy ending” was, and then asked for my number. I gave him the number to a Domino’s instead. I was so drunk that whole trip too. I forgot all about it but I still remember his face. Ew"Others pointed to patterns. Yuyu stated:"i talked to sykkuno in february after he followed me and then i started ignoring him bc i clocked him. y he was 34 and i was like 18…yeah bro is rly doing this on a massive scale 😭."Chysai added:"Damn. Guess I was just another number on his list then. Feels bad man… 😭😭 He messaged me on X and I thought it wasn’t even real, like his account got hacked or something. Our convo moved to Discord and the moment I mentioned I was in a relationship, he went radio silent shortly after. I thought he was genuinely trying to make more friends… I’m so stupid! 😫I feel like I can’t trust anyone in the Vtuber space these days. 🥹."Meanwhile, Nami described a moment that crossed her comfort line:"After some more drinks, we had to go back to my apartment to pick up the prizes. He wanted to stay and play games or watch a show with me.. I didn’t feel comfortable and politely thanked him for the night. Sykunno grabbed me by the waist and pulled me in against his body and asked in a flirty tone 'so, do you want something'. ATP I was really nervous but I just told him no sorry but thank you for the fun and to have a good night."As the conversation grows louder, the absence of a direct response keeps the story unresolved, and the internet watching closely.
Article Summary: Streaming star Sykkuno accused of manipulation as claims flood social media. Several women claim patterns of dishonesty and manipulation. The allegations, grave and very personal, have not been addressed publicly so far by the streamer. The controversy continues to grow, raising questions about accountability, influence, and trust within the streaming community.

Article Title: Quote of the day by Abraham Lincoln: “Character is like a tree, and reputation like its shadow. The shadow is what we think of it; the tree is the real thing.”
Article Text:
Quote of the day by Abraham Lincoln

Quote of the day by ex-US President Abraham Lincoln



“Character is like a tree, and reputation like its shadow. The shadow is what we think of it; the tree is the real thing.”



Understanding the meaning of Abraham Lincoln’s quote



Why character matters more than reputation



How reputation is shaped in society



The role of actions in building character



Real-life relevance of the quote



A simple comparison that explains a complex truth



Other famous quotes by Abraham Lincoln



“Government of the people, by the people, for the people, shall not perish from the Earth.”

“Those who deny freedom to others deserve it not for themselves.”

“I am a slow walker, but I never walk back.”

“Whatever you are, be a good one.”

Why this message still matters today



A takeaway from Lincoln’s perspective









People are often judged by what others see or hear about them in their daily lives. The way people see and think about someone can affect how they are treated in society. But this view isn't always a full picture of who they really are. This is where Abraham Lincoln's quote comes in handy. It makes it clear what is real and what is not.Abraham Lincoln was a great leader during one of the hardest times in American history. He often spoke in simple but important ways. His words are still important today because they talk about basic human values. He uses a simple comparison to make a complicated idea easier to understand. He says that character is like a tree and reputation is like its shadow. They are both connected, but they are not the same.This idea is simple to understand, but it has a lot of meaning. It reminds people that what other people think isn't always the whole truth. It also shows how important it is to pay more attention to your inner values than your outer image. Let's look into what this quote means and why it still matters today.The quote uses a picture of nature to explain a human idea. A tree is solid, real, and has depth.It gets bigger over time and stands for something that exists on its own. A shadow, on the other hand, is just a reflection. It changes depending on the light, the angle, and the point of view.Lincoln is talking about a person's true nature when he compares character to a tree. Honesty, integrity, and actions that show who a person really is are all parts of character. It grows over time and is built by doing the same things over and over again.He says that reputation is like a shadow that shows how other people see a person. It can change quickly based on what people think, what they hear, or what they don't know. It might not always show the whole picture, just like a shadow.People often care more about their reputation because it's easy to see. It has an effect on how people accept you, how many job opportunities you have, and how people see you in public. But sometimes, reputation can be wrong.Character, on the other hand, stays the same even when no one is watching. It helps people make choices and do things. Lincoln's quote makes the point that real value is in character, not in what other people think of it.This idea is important because it moves the focus from how things look to how they really are. It encourages people to develop strong values instead of just worrying about how they look.People often build their reputations through how they act in public, how they talk to others, and how they interact with others. There are many things that can affect it, such as the media, social networks, and personal relationships.Reputation can change quickly because it is based on how people see things. An event or misunderstanding can change how people see someone. This is like how a shadow changes when the light changes.Lincoln's comparison elucidates why reputation ought not to be regarded as the sole criterion for evaluating an individual. It is just one part, and it might not always be right.Character is not built in a single moment. It grows over time through regular actions. Every choice, big or small, affects who a person is.Being honest with people you see every day, being responsible at work, and treating others with respect are all parts of character. These traits may not be obvious right away, but they will shape a person over time.Lincoln's quote reminds us that character is what matters most. Reputation may follow, but it is not the same as what is really going on.You can use this idea in many parts of your life. People are often judged at work based on how well they do their jobs and how others see them. Reputation can help you get ahead, but character is what will help you succeed in the long run.In personal relationships, trust is built by doing the same things over and over again. First impressions can shape people's opinions, but over time, true character shows through.People often judge leaders based on their reputation in public life. But history usually remembers them for who they were and what they did.One reason this quote is still popular is that it's simple. It's easy to understand the picture of a tree and its shadow. But it does a good job of showing the difference between what is real and what we think is real.A tree is there even if it casts a shadow. The tree stays the same, even if the shadow changes or goes away. In the same way, a person's character is separate from their reputation.This comparison helps people see that what other people think of them doesn't define who they really are.Reputation can change in a flash in today's world, where information spreads quickly. Social media and other digital platforms make it easy for people to quickly form opinions. In a situation like this, the difference between character and reputation becomes even more important.Lincoln's quote reminds us that reputation can change how people see us, but it doesn't define who we are. The true worth of a person is in their actions, choices, and honesty.This message is still important because it talks about a basic human need. People want to be known for who they really are, not just how others see them.The quote makes a clear point. Character is what really makes a person who they are, while reputation is just what other people think of them. They are both related, but they are not the same.People can create a strong and stable identity by focusing on their character. Your character stays the same, but your reputation can change over time.Abraham Lincoln's words make it easy to see the difference. They tell people to look beyond how they look and focus on what really makes them who they are.
Article Summary: Lincoln's quote makes the point that real value is in character, not in what other people think of it. Reputation may follow, but it is not the same as what is really going on. Honesty, integrity, and actions that show who a person really is are all parts of character.

Article Title: Quote of the day by Princess Diana: “Nothing brings me more happiness than trying to help the most vulnerable people in society. It is a goal and an essential part of my life—a kind of destiny. Whoeve
Article Text:
Quote of the day by Princess Diana

Quote of the day by Princess Diana

“Nothing brings me more happiness than trying to help the most vulnerable people in society. It is a goal and an essential part of my life—a kind of destiny. Whoever is in distress can call on me. I will come running wherever they are.”

Watch Princess Diana’s Biggest Regret: BBC Interview ‘HURT MY BOYS,’ Insider Reveal | WATCH Princess Diana’s Biggest Regret: BBC Interview ‘HURT MY BOYS,’ Insider Reveal | WATCH

Understanding the meaning behind the quote by Princess Diana

Princess Diana’s humanitarian approach

Why this quote by Princess Diana continues to be shared today

Princess Diana: Public image and global recognition

Emotional impact of her words

Legacy of compassion in public memory

Other famous quotes by Princess Diana

“Family is the most important thing in the world.”

“Only do what your heart tells you.”

“Anywhere I see suffering, that is where I want to be, doing what I can.”

“Hugs can do great amounts of good—especially for children.”

“People think that at the end of the day a man is the only answer to fulfillment. Actually, a job is better for me.”

“I like to be a free spirit. Some don’t like that, but that’s the way I am.”

“Carry out a random act of kindness, with no expectation of reward, safe in the knowledge that one day someone might do the same for you.”

Influence on modern humanitarian thinking







People all over the world remember Princess Diana not only as a member of the British royal family but also as a humanitarian who always helped people in need. She was known for working directly with charities, hospitals, and at-risk communities. She often talked about how important it is to be kind, help others, and have strong emotional connections with people who are going through tough times. One of her most famous quotes shows how deeply human she was. The quote shows that helping people in need was a big part of who she was and what she did, not just something she did once in a while. Even years after she died, her words are still shared a lot on social media, news sites, and in public discussions. This is especially true on days when people share inspirational quotes. This quote still resonates because it is simple, clear about emotions, and focuses on human dignity.This quote shows a consistent theme in Diana's public life: her dedication to helping people who were having trouble with their bodies, minds, or relationships. It also shows that she didn't just see humanitarian work as a job; she saw it as a calling.The quote has three main ideas that make up its message.First, it shows how helping others can make you feel good emotionally. Diana says that helping weak people makes her happier than anything else. This shows a point of view that helping others is a way to feel fulfilled.Second, she says that helping other people is both a goal and a "kind of destiny." This wording makes it sound like she saw compassion as a long-term goal in life, not just a passing interest.Third, the statement stresses the need to act quickly when someone is in trouble. The phrase "I will come running wherever they are" shows that you need to act quickly and be available. It suggests that you should be hands-on instead of distant or symbolic.Princess Diana was well-known for her work on humanitarian issues throughout her public life. She didn't do the usual royal duties that were mostly about looking good in public. Instead, she went to hospitals, shelters, and communities that were directly affected by illness or poverty a lot.She supported a lot of different causes, like getting rid of landmines, helping kids, and helping people who are homeless. One of the most famous things she did was choose to physically interact with HIV/AIDS patients when there was a lot of wrong information and stigma. This method helped change how people thought about things in the late 20th century.People often said that she got involved in a direct and personal way. She chose to speak, listen, and physically comfort people during visits instead of keeping her distance. This helped her make her point that compassion should be active, not passive.The fact that this quote is still being shared on digital platforms shows how simple and universal it is. When people talk about mental health, social inequality, and community support these days, they often use the phrase in motivational and awareness contexts.The quote is still important because it doesn't talk about power or status. Instead, it focuses on making things easier to get to and connecting with other people. People who want to help others "wherever they are" have been seen as having limitless empathy.In the media, these kinds of quotes are often used in "quote of the day" sections, especially in articles that try to show how compassion and leadership have changed over time.People all over the world knew Princess Diana while she was alive because she was a royal and a good person. She broke the usual royal rules when it came to talking to people; instead of keeping a formal distance, she often tried to connect with them on an emotional level.She became much more famous around the world in the 1980s and 1990s, when her work for charities got a lot of media attention. She got involved with causes that hurt people directly, especially those who were looked down on or misunderstood at the time.She died in 1997, but her legacy lives on through charitable foundations that are still going strong, public memory, and frequent mentions in news stories around the world.This quote is powerful because it is short and to the point. It doesn't use words or ideas that are hard to understand or that aren't real. Instead, it focuses on direct human experience—helping people who need it and responding to distress.Many people read the quote and think of it as a reminder of their duty to society. It also sends a bigger message about empathy being a choice you make, not just a feeling you have.Because of this, the quote is often used to raise awareness about charity, promote volunteer work, and send inspirational messages.Princess Diana's humanitarian work is a big part of her legacy. Her work in public helped bring up problems that weren't talked about much in mainstream society at the time.People often say that her work to help people with HIV/AIDS and her work to stop landmines are two of the most important things she has done. These activities helped raise awareness among the general public and got people talking more about health, dignity, and global responsibility.This quote shows this larger legacy by stressing how important it is for people to be able to respond quickly and emotionally to those in need.When people talk about empathy-driven leadership, they still talk about Princess Diana's public life and words. Her method showed that being visible and caring can work together to get people to pay attention to social issues.People often use her words as examples of how public figures can directly help vulnerable groups in modern humanitarian discourse. The idea of accessibility—being willing to help someone in need without any barriers—continues to be a big part of her legacy.One of Princess Diana's most famous quotes is, “Nothing brings me more happiness than trying to help the most vulnerable people in society.” It shows how she lived her life, which was based on kindness, openness, and helping others. The statement is still true today because it talks about values that are important to all people, no matter where or when they live. It goes around in modern media as "quote of the day" features, which reinforces the idea that helping others is not only a nice thing to do, but also a very important part of being human.
Article Summary: Princess Diana was well-known for her work on humanitarian issues throughout her public life. She was known for working directly with charities, hospitals, and at-risk communities. She often talked about how important it is to be kind, help others, and have strong emotional connections with people who are going through tough times.

Article Title: Russia News: ‘One in 15.5 million’: Woman in Russia gives birth to four identical girls
Article Text:
Woman in Russia gives birth to four identical girls/Image: RT







A rare and medically significant birth has been reported in St Petersburg, where a woman delivered four identical baby girls, marking the first such case officially recorded in Russia.The delivery, which took place at a maternity hospital in the city, has drawn attention due to its extreme rarity. Medical estimates suggest that monochorionic, or identical, quadruplets occur in around one in 15.5 million births. Globally, only about 15 such cases have been documented.All four babies were born at 32 weeks of pregnancy and are reported to be in stable condition. Their birth weights ranged between 1,360g and 1,640g, and their lengths were between 37cm and 41cm. Doctors described these figures as strong for babies born at this stage.In a statement shared by the hospital on Russia’s social media platform VKontakte , the team said, “Four charming sisters were born at 32 weeks. According to estimates, monochorionic (identical) quadruplets occur once in 15.5 million births. Such unique births have not been reported in the country.”What makes the case particularly complex is that all four babies developed from a single fertilised egg and shared one placenta, a condition known as a monochorionic pregnancy.In most multiple pregnancies, each baby has a separate placenta. Doctors note that such cases carry higher risks and require continuous monitoring.The hospital added, “For a 32-week pregnancy, this is an excellent result, made possible by the mother's proper management in the antenatal department and the expertise of the doctors.”The delivery required the coordinated effort of a large medical team, including obstetricians, anaesthesiologists, intensive care specialists, midwives, and neonatal experts. Each baby was immediately attended to by neonatologists and specialised nurses to ensure proper care from the first moments after birth.According to the hospital, the procedure was led by chief physician Professor Anton Mikhailov, along with a team of specialists from the prenatal department and obstetrics unit. The operation was described as highly complex, requiring precision and teamwork throughout.Doctors explained that identical, or monozygotic, pregnancies occur when a single fertilised egg splits into multiple embryos, resulting in babies with identical genetic makeup and the same sex. There is no known way to predict or prevent such occurrences.The case is now being seen as a milestone in Russian medical practice, highlighting both the rarity of the event and the level of care required to manage such a high-risk pregnancy successfully.
Article Summary: A woman in St Petersburg gave birth to four identical girls at 32 weeks. It is the first known case of monochorionic quadruplets in Russia. All four babies developed from a single fertilised egg and shared one placenta. The delivery required the coordinated effort of a large medical team.

Article Title: Indian man in Auckland faces abusive calls after video of him dumping trash from car goes viral: 'I already apologised'
Article Text: An Indian-origin man in Auckland says he has filed complaints with police and sought help from online safety authorities after a video of him allegedly dumping cardboard went viral, leading to abusive messages and public backlash.The man (Name withheld) says the incident has left him feeling unsafe after the footage was shared on social media. He also claims the person who filmed the confrontation damaged his vehicle during the episode and broke a rearview mirror.“I have apologised to the Police, I also told them that I am ready to pay the fine (if any) and apologised to the person who made our video,” the man told The Indian Weekender.Police have confirmed they received a complaint linked to the incident, which took place on April 4 in Alfriston, Auckland, reports the Indian Weekender. A spokesperson said, “Police can confirm they received a report in relation to an incident in Alfriston earlier this month."The man says he and a friend were attempting to dispose of three large cardboard pieces ahead of a planned function at their home. He claims they checked recycling options in Manurewa and Papakura, but found the facilities closed due to a public holiday.On their return, they tried to leave the cardboard in a grassy area, believing it would break down over time, before being confronted by a passing motorist.“Within 15 seconds, a car stopped in front of ours and a man got out with a phone camera, started recording and abusing,” the man said.The confrontation became tense on video, with the recording person shouting, “Don’t come to our country and litter you m**”. The footage also captures the recorded abuse.The video begins with the recorder saying, “You f** A**”, and shows the man apologising and returning the cardboard to his vehicle after being confronted. In response, the recorder continues, “Don’t be sorry. Don’t come to our country and litter you m**”.The clip has since gone viral, attracting thousands of reactions and comments.The man says he has also approached Netsafe for assistance in removing the video from social media. However, he claims the organisation has been limited in what it can do.“Netsafe told me that they will not be able to remove all the videos since the video is not against community standards,” the man said.“The video is viral in India as well. Netsafe has only been able to remove a few videos,” he added.He says that the online spread of the footage has been driven by pages seeking engagement and views, and says he regrets the incident.“I want to let the people know if you do something wrong, accept and apologise immediately,” he said.
Article Summary: An Indian-origin man in Auckland says he has filed complaints with police and sought help from online safety authorities after a video of him allegedly dumping cardboard went viral. The man says the incident has left him feeling unsafe after the footage was shared on social media. He claims the person who filmed the confrontation damaged his vehicle during the episode and broke a rearview mirror.

Article Title: Who is Sykkuno? Popular streamer faces explosive allegations as exposé shakes ‘wholesome’ image
Article Text:
Sykkuno faces serious allegations after HemomalVT accused him of manipulation, cheating, and exploiting smaller creators through a detailed exposé with screenshots and audio clips. Claims suggest he maintained multiple relationships while hiding a long-term girlfriend. Another creator, Elfilea, was also mentioned. With no response from Sykkuno yet, the controversy has divided fans and shaken his long-standing wholesome image.

Allegations, evidence, and a growing divide among fans







The internet does not calm down easily, and this time the media attention has shifted a glaring eye towards Sykkino. With his smooth voice and drama-free legacy, the mainstream producer has been in the storm of the limelight and the fans are more confused, divided and in need of an answer. What began as a one-time blog has rapidly escalated into a full-fledged controversy, drawing in previous experiences and casting serious doubt upon the faith in cyberspace.Things got out of control when HemomalVT posted a detailed document online, claiming that the streamer was manipulative. Supported by screen shots, audio files, and first-hand stories, the allegations have become the subject of heated discussion in the gaming community. As the story is being covered by millions of people in real-time, the previously apparent image of an innocent and peaceful creator suddenly appears much more complex.At the center of the controversy is a lengthy document that outlines what Hemomal describes as a pattern of emotional manipulation. She claims that her connection with Sykkuno moved unusually fast, shifting from casual chats to deeply personal conversations in a short span.According to her, the situation became more troubling when she discovered he was not single during their interactions.In her own words, she wrote:"He (Sykkuno) had been my role model and someone I looked up to for a long time... However, what I learned about Sykkuno after that day would make me regret my decision. Sykkuno is not the kind, soft-spoken character you see on stream. He is a serial cheater, liar, and manipulator who uses his status to get what he wants from as many women as possible, more specifically, ones who are smaller or up-and-coming content creators, knowing that he can take advantage of them. "The claims do not stop there. Hemomal also pointed to conversations with another creator, Elfilea, suggesting similar experiences. She added:"After speaking to Elfilea a few days ago, we were able to corroborate our experiences with Sykkuno and learn that we were both victims of his... Sykkuno is known to send hundreds of dollars to the girls he wants to get involved with. This screenshot is from when he took my friend Elfilea to Paris during the 2025 TFT tournament, along with sending her 500 euros to help her move into her new apartment."She further claimed:"After Elfilea’s Paris trip, and 2 weeks after he had started talking to me, saying things like how he wants to move in with me and such, he would message Elfilea asking her if she’ll be at Offkai. This is proof that he’d be talking to more than one woman at the same time, planning a hook-up for every event he went to. He was doing this while giving me the impression that he wanted something long-term, and all while also having a girlfriend since 2021. "As of now, Sykkuno has not publicly responded. The silence has only deepened the divide. Some fans are urging caution and waiting for his side, while others believe the volume of claims cannot be ignored. In the fast-moving world of streaming, reputation can take years to build but only moments to question.
Article Summary: Sykkuno accused of manipulation, cheating, and exploiting smaller creators. Claims suggest he maintained multiple relationships while hiding a long-term girlfriend. Another creator, Elfilea, was also mentioned. The controversy has divided fans and shaken his long-standing wholesome image.

Article Title: Indian Students Performance: Watch: Indian students play UAE national anthem on musical instruments in emotional tribute, clip wins hearts online
Article Text:
What inspired the idea?

Social media reactions







A video going viral on the internet has stolen hearts online for the immense patriotism and love it displays for the United Arab Emirates and gratitude for its devotion towards its residents. 13 Indian students belonging to expat families in the emirate gathered to perform the country's national anthem Ishy Bilady (Long Live My Country), in a moment that brought tears to many eyes.The clip, created by Dubai-based performing arts centre Malhaar, featured schoolchildren using Indian instruments such as the tabla, sitar, harmonium and others to create a musical rendition of the national anthem. The centre labelled it as an "offering of harmony, friendship and shared cultural respect."The idea that originated as a simple creative thought transformed into a national moment, gaining recognition from institutional pages and authorities including the Knowledge and Human Development Authority (KHDA). The video quickly gained traction, even being shared by India's Ambassador to the UAE and the Embassy of India in Abu Dhabi. "I've received calls from CEOs, homemakers, teachers and principals, it has really resonated with them, said Jogiraj Sikidar, Founder and Director of the centre.As per Sikidar, a line by the UAE President and ruler Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan served as the inspiration behind the production. “Soon after the war started, I was driving and noticed a hoarding that said, ‘In the UAE, we are all Emirati’, and that really stayed with me. I have been in the UAE for 20 years, and while many call it their second home, I call it my first. I feel deeply rooted in this country; it has given me so much,” he said.Soon, the idea for the video was born and executed along with a third-generation UAE friend who brought the project to life. They kept the clothes all-white with the only colours representing the country's flag. "We did not use any electronic instruments or visual or audio effects — just acoustic instruments played by the children," shared Sikidar.Numerous people on the internet commended the video, appreciating the spirit of nationalism within the children who so dedicatedly performed the instrumental melody."Soul-stirring! Indian kids blending tabla & sitar into "Ishy Bilady" pure harmony! India stands shoulder-to-shoulder with the Emirates: joint Desert Cyclone exercises, counter-terror intel sharing & strategic defence partnership for shared peace & security. Family forged in sand & steel-together, unbreakable!" wrote a user on X (formerly Twitter)."God bless UAE... Thank you for this wonderful performance... It shows how much we love UAE" added another."Art and music are so important in this world, especially in the current situation. Music makes you calmer, rational, and at peace. Music brings souls together, making this world a better place to live. Congratulations to these children," one wrote.The national anthem of UAE was officially adopted in December 1971 following the formation of the country. While the lyrics were written by Dr. Aref Al Sheikh Abdullah Al Hassan in 1986, the music was composed by Egyptian composer Mohammed Abdel Wahab in 1971.
Article Summary: 13 Indian students belonging to expat families in the emirate gathered to perform the country's national anthem Ishy Bilady. The clip, created by Dubai- based performing arts centre Malhaar, featured schoolchildren using Indian instruments such as the tabla, sitar, harmonium and others.

Article Title: UK’s most notorious prisoner Charles Bronson moved to high-security ‘Monster Mansion’ before parole bid
Article Text:
Charles Bronson, the infamous 73-year-old inmate, has been relocated to the maximum-security facility known as HMP Wakefield, often referred to as 'Monster Mansion,' just in time for a pivotal parole hearing. Sources within the prison indicate heightened tension as staff express concerns for his well-being after his art materials were taken away and he was isolated.

Who is Charles Bronson?







Britain's most notorious prisoner Charles Bronson has been moved to HMP Wakefield, known as Monster Mansion, at a time when he is preparing for what could be his final attempt to secure release after more than 50 years behind bars.Bronson, 73, was transferred last week from Long Lartin prison in Worcestershire to the high-security facility in West Yorkshire, which houses some of the country's most dangerous convicted criminals. Prison officers at Wakefield are said to be deeply worried about his placement there after he was put back into solitary confinement and had his art materials taken away.A source familiar with the situation said nobody inside the prison could understand why the transfer had happened at such a sensitive time. The source said Bronson's passion for painting is well known and that removing his pens and paints has raised fears he will react badly to the conditions.His ex-wife Irene Dunroe, 63, who remains in regular contact with Bronson and refers to him by his real name Mick Peterson, said she was appalled by the decision. She said he had been placed in a cell within a cell in an underground section of the prison. She described the move as a deliberate attempt to unsettle him ahead of his oral parole hearing and said prison authorities were "stretching the elastic to see how far they can make it break.That hearing marks a significant step forward in his case. Earlier this year his application moved beyond a written assessment to a full hearing where witnesses can be questioned and evidence examined in person. Bronson himself has expressed deep frustration with the process, telling a British outlet that he has been held beyond his tariff for more than two decades and continues to be denied any meaningful progress towards release.Bronson was born Michael Gordon Peterson in 1952. He received his first prison sentence at 22 for armed robbery and was freed in 1987 before returning to custody the following year after committing another offence. Violence against staff and inmates repeatedly added years to his sentence. A hostage-taking incident involving a prison art teacher in 1999 led to a life term and he was convicted of assaulting a prison governor as recently as 2014.It was during an earlier stay at Wakefield that officers persuaded him to try drawing. That introduction to art eventually became central to his identity. He changed his name to Charles Salvador in 2014 in honour of the artists who inspired him and later set up a foundation to promote his work and support others in difficult circumstances.He has now spent more than 12 years without a violent incident which those in his corner see as proof of real change. The Parole Board rejected his application in 2023 and this upcoming hearing is his ninth attempt to walk free.Wakefield also holds serial killer Robert Maudsley. Soham murderer Ian Huntley spent time there before dying earlier this year after being attacked by another inmate.The Ministry of Justice declined to comment on his case.
Article Summary: Bronson, 73, was transferred last week from Long Lartin prison in Worcestershire to the high-security facility in West Yorkshire. Prison officers at Wakefield are said to be deeply worried about his placement there after he was put back into solitary confinement and had his art materials taken away.

Article Title: UK weather forecast: April warmth highlights climate shifts in Britain; seasonal temperatures expected to return as weather patterns shift
Article Text:
UK weather forecast: April warmth highlights climate shifts in Britain; seasonal temperatures expected to return as weather patterns shift







A spike of unseasonable warmth swept over Britain, peaking midweek when readings hit 26.6°C amid the trees of Kew Gardens, among the hottest April starts ever seen. Despite the sunshine tease, meteorologists point to looming Atlantic fronts set to drag in wet skies, gusting winds, colder air rolling in by Saturday.Wednesday turned out hotter than any other day this year, the Met Office said, making it the second hottest early April in history. Not long before, Mona saw mercury hit 24.8°C, unusual warmth for April seventh, breaking its own past mark. Places across the UK ran warmer than spots like Rome or Ibiza at the same time, records show. That kind of heat surprised even seasoned weather watchers.From southern areas of continental Europe, winds carried unseasonal heat toward the UK, resulting in readings over 10°C higher than normal across multiple locations, according to Ben Rich at the Met Office. Though such patterns have occurred before, today's baseline warmth, shaped by long-term shifts, is making extremes more pronounced, he noted. Instead of isolated anomalies, current conditions reflect a broader trend where past temperature limits are increasingly exceeded. Due to altered global systems, even typical weather movements now yield stronger outcomes than seen decades ago. While natural variability plays a role, underlying changes raise the floor on how cold it can get, and push highs further still.Weather predictions show clear changes ahead. From the Atlantic, westerly winds move in, carrying lower temperatures and extensive rainfall across the British Isles. Though parts of southeast England could reach 22°C by Thursday, such warmth fades quickly. As colder air expands, many areas adjust toward typical levels, between 10°C and 14°C. The pattern holds firm through the coming days.From the west, rain bands advance without pause, reaching widespread areas. By Friday afternoon, downpours arrive in Northern Ireland, while coastal stretches there and in western Scotland face gusts ranging 50 to 70 mph. Where cold air meets wet systems, higher terrain across Scotland encounters winter weather, sleet or snow possible. Despite milder flows elsewhere, elevation brings change. Conditions shift gradually as moisture persists.Falling fast after dark, night-time air chills most over northern regions and parts of the west. Where skies stay open overhead by dawn Friday, a sharp cool settles into low spots. Ground warmth slips away quickly under empty cloud cover, making mornings seem sharper than before.Expect changeable weather over the weekend, shifting between gloomy stretches and clearer moments. In northern zones, powerful winds arrive now and then, interrupting calmer periods. Rain moves across several locations, sweeping through in brief bursts rather than steady downpours. Meanwhile, southern territories see extended breaks without precipitation, sunlight filtering through at times. Eastern districts remain relatively free of cloud cover, though interruptions occur when systems advance from upstream areas.A slow rise in temperature is forecast once early-week coolness fades. Following this, spring-like patterns are likely to reappear. Despite a short spell of unusual heat, seasonal rhythms seem to be resuming. That shift reveals how local weather dances around long-term shifts in Earth's climate system.
Article Summary: Temperatures hit 26.6°C in Kew Gardens, among the hottest April starts ever seen. Forecasters point to looming Atlantic fronts set to drag in wet skies, gusting winds, colder air rolling in by Saturday. From the Atlantic, westerly winds move in, carrying lower temperatures and extensive rainfall across Britain.
